era_context: Second Intermediate Period in Egypt; Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; mature Indus Valley Civilization; Hittite Old Kingdom; early Shang dynasty in China (traditional dating).

events.title: Samsu-iluna's reign in Babylon
events.description: Samsu-iluna continued to rule Babylon. Administrative records from this period document the functioning of the Old Babylonian state, including temple management, legal disputes, and economic transactions. The reign was characterized by efforts to maintain stability amid external pressures.
events.certainty_note: Attested in cuneiform sources, but absolute dating uncertain.

historiographic_note: This year is not distinguished by any specific documented event in available sources. The historical record for this period is characterized by continuity rather than dramatic change. Most information comes from administrative documents that do not provide year-by-year narrative.
